hunt_type: §6.N.1 per-invocation hunt: 5 random *Test.kt + 2 gate-shaping production files
trigger: Autonomous loop continuation after 32nd §6.L invocation cycle. Per §6.N.1.1 baseline cadence: every operator anti-bluff-mandate invocation triggers a 5+2 hunt.

checks_applied: @Test count > 0 (otherwise no test bodies to verify)
mock_only_verify count: high count + low assertion_calls would indicate count-only-assertion bluff
mock_the_SUT pattern: mockk<X>(...) where X matches the test class name root — Forbidden Test Pattern per Seventh Law clause 4
@Ignore without issue citation: would be a §6.L violation

results.verification_method: Indirect — ran tests/firebase/run_all.sh which exercises the 3 §6.P gates + Phase 1 Gates 4+5 + scripts/firebase-*.sh no-warn-swallow
results.verdict: Production-gate intact — the firebase-distribute.sh enforces §6.P + §6.AA constraints

results.verification_method: Indirect — ran tests/pre-push/check{4,5,6,7,8,9}_test.sh which exercise Checks 4 (§6.N.1.2) + 5 (§6.N.1.3) + 6 (§6.Y) + 7 (§6.Z) + 8 (§11.4.17) + 9 (CM-SCRIPT-DOCS-SYNC)
results.verdict: Pre-push hook intact — all 9 Checks fire per their respective hermetic-test contracts

full_hermetic_run.scope: all hermetic test suites under tests/*

audit_result.verdict: NO BLUFFS detected in the §6.N.1 hunt. 5 random tests CLEAN. 2 gate-shaping production files VERIFIED INTACT via their hermetic test suites. 19/19 total hermetic tests PASS.

constitutional_implications: This hunt closes the §6.N.1.1 baseline cadence requirement for the 32nd §6.L cycle. Combined with the prior 32nd-cycle work (matrix-runner actual execution, ::ComposableFn bluff caught, macOS-emulator-stall incident, §6.AB Layer 2 enforcement enhancement, Challenge-body structural audit), this completes the 32nd-cycle anti-bluff sweep.
follow_up_owed: Per §6.N.1.1 future-cycle rule: subsequent same-day operator invocations trigger lighter incident-response hunts (1-2 files). The 32nd-cycle hunt (this file) was full 5+2 because it's the first hunt of the cycle.

Per §6.N.2 production-code coverage rule: bluff hunts MUST sample production code beyond gate-shaping files. The 2 sampled here (firebase-distribute.sh + pre-push) are both gate-shaping; broader CI-touched code sampling is owed in subsequent cycles.
